hero.subtitle: Ergonomics, aesthetics, and attention to detail. A space that inspires you to create great projects.
section.title: Thought through to the smallest details
card.ergonomics.title: Ergonomics
card.ergonomics.desc: Lighting, screen position, back health — everything to work without fatigue.
card.devices.title: Devices
card.devices.desc: Mechanical keyboards, mice, monitors — tactile pleasure from every keystroke.
card.checklist.title: Checklist
card.checklist.desc: A ready-made plan: from software to cable management. Just follow the steps.
quote.text: "The environment you work in affects the quality of your code just as much as your tech stack."
quote.author: — Dream Setup Philosophy
features.title: Workspace Ergonomics
features.subtitle: How to organize your workspace to maintain health and boost productivity for years to come.
features.lighting.title: Proper Lighting
features.lighting.item1: Use warm light (2700-3000K) in the evening
features.lighting.item2: The monitor should not be the only light source
features.lighting.item3: Bias lighting behind the monitor reduces eye strain
features.posture.title: Body Position
features.posture.item1: Top of the monitor at eye level
features.posture.item3: Feet flat on the floor or on a footrest
features.cables.title: Cable Management
features.cables.item1: Cable channels and ties are a must for cleanliness
features.breaks.title: Work Routine
features.breaks.item2: 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes look into the distance
features.breaks.item3: Neck and back stretches every hour
features.image.title: Invest in Yourself
features.image.desc: A good chair and height-adjustable desk pay off with back health after a year of use.
devices.title: Choosing Devices
devices.subtitle: Tools that turn work into pleasure. Tactile sensations matter.
devices.keyboard.title: Keychron Q1 Pro
devices.keyboard.desc: Aluminum case, lubed stabilizers, PBT keycaps
devices.monitor.title: LG UltraWide 40"
devices.monitor.desc: 5120x2160, IPS Black, 95W Power Delivery via USB-C
devices.mouse.title: Logitech MX Master 3S
devices.mouse.desc: Quiet clicks, magnetic scroll, multi-device support
devices.headphones.title: Sony WH-1000XM5
devices.headphones.desc: Best noise cancellation, 30 hours battery, all-day comfort
devices.advice.title: How to choose devices?
devices.advice1.title: Priority: Tactility
devices.advice1.desc: Keyboard and mouse are what you constantly interact with. Don't skimp.
devices.advice2.title: One Big Monitor
devices.advice2.desc: Better than two medium ones. Fewer bezels, more comfortable for coding.
devices.advice3.title: Wireless Setup
devices.advice3.desc: One receiver for all devices (Logi Bolt or Bluetooth).
setup.title: Setup Checklist
setup.subtitle: A step-by-step plan to create the perfect workspace. Check off completed items.
setup.hardware.title: ⚙️ Hardware
setup.hardware.item3: Monitor at eye level (stand or arm)
setup.software.title: 💻 Software
setup.software.item1: Editor theme and color scheme configured (dark mode)
setup.software.item2: f.lux or built-in night mode installed
setup.software.item3: Window manager (yabai, i3, Rectangle for macOS)
setup.atmosphere.title: 🌿 Atmosphere
setup.progress.label: Setup Progress
setup.progress.hint: ✓ Check items — progress is automatically saved
keyboard.title: Choosing a Keyboard
keyboard.subtitle: A mechanical keyboard is not just a typing tool. It's tactile pleasure, customization, and full control over every keystroke. Programmers spend thousands of hours on it.
keyboard.desc: 75% mechanical keyboard with aluminum case, hot-swappable switches, and wireless connectivity. The benchmark for programmers and enthusiasts.
keyboard.benefit1.title: Tactility
keyboard.benefit1.desc: Every keystroke is felt by your fingers
keyboard.benefit2.title: Customization
keyboard.benefit3.title: Durability
keyboard.benefit4.title: Speed
keyboard.switch.title: Switch Types
keyboard.switch.linear.desc: Smooth travel without click or tactile bump. For gaming and fast typing.
keyboard.switch.tactile.desc: Light tactile bump without click. Versatile choice for coding.
keyboard.switch.clicky.desc: Loud click and tactile feedback. For those who like to hear every character.
keyboard.pros1: Aluminum case — no flex or creaking
keyboard.pros2: Ready to use out of the box (lubed stabilizers)
keyboard.pros4: QMK/VIA support — full layer and macro configuration
keyboard.review1.name: Artem, backend dev
keyboard.review1.text: Switched from a membrane keyboard a year ago. Now I can't type on regular ones — my fingers remember this pleasant tactile feedback. Code writes itself.
keyboard.review2.name: Elena, frontend dev
keyboard.review2.text: Configured layers in VIA: standard layout on one, arrows and navigation under the right hand on the other. No need to reach.
keyboard.review3.name: Denis, tech lead
keyboard.review3.text: Bought the same keyboard for home and work. Muscle memory doesn't have to adjust, comfortable everywhere. Colleagues also wanted mechanicals.
keyboard.combo.title: Popular Switch Combos
keyboard.combo1.title: For Programming
keyboard.combo1.desc: Gateron Brown + Krytox 205g0 lube. Balance of tactility and quietness.
keyboard.combo2.title: For Office
keyboard.combo2.desc: Gateron Silent Red + O-rings. Max quiet, doesn't annoy colleagues.
keyboard.combo3.title: For Tactile Bliss
keyboard.combo3.desc: Holy Panda or Gateron Baby Kangaroo. Pronounced tactile bump.
mouse.title: Choosing a Mouse
mouse.subtitle: The mouse is one of the main tools for a programmer. Its ergonomics affect wrist health and work comfort.
mouse.desc: The ergonomic benchmark for programmers and designers. Quiet clicks, magnetic scroll, and support for up to 3 devices simultaneously.
mouse.specs.sensor.title: Sensor
mouse.specs.sensor.desc: Darkfield 4000 DPI, works on glass and any surface
mouse.specs.battery.title: Battery
mouse.specs.battery.desc: Up to 70 days of use, fast charging (1 minute = 3 hours)
mouse.specs.connect.title: Connection
mouse.specs.connect.desc: Bluetooth + Logi Bolt, switch between 3 devices
mouse.cons2: A bit heavy for fast gaming
mouse.cons3: USB-C only for charging, not data
monitor.title: Choosing a Monitor
monitor.subtitle: The monitor is the window to your code. Its quality affects not only work enjoyment but also eye health. Ultrawide formats change the perception of productivity.
monitor.desc: 40-inch 5K2K Nano IPS monitor with 21:9 aspect ratio. Replaces two 27-inch monitors with no bezel in the middle. Ideal for programming and video editing.
monitor.benefit1.desc: Two code windows side by side without scrolling
monitor.benefit2.title: 5K2K
monitor.benefit3.title: USB-C
monitor.benefit3.desc: 95W laptop charging via a single cable
monitor.benefit4.title: Eye Care
monitor.pros1: Massive workspace — 3 windows side by side
monitor.pros2: Single USB-C cable for everything (video + charging)
monitor.cons2: Requires a powerful GPU for gaming
monitor.cons3: Takes a lot of desk space (depth)
headphones.title: Choosing Headphones
headphones.subtitle: Good noise-cancelling headphones are a necessity for productive work. They create personal space even in a noisy office or cafe and help you focus on code.
headphones.desc: The benchmark for wireless noise-cancelling headphones. 8th generation noise-cancelling processor, 30 hours battery life, and incredible comfort for long work sessions.
headphones.benefit1.title: Noise Cancelling
headphones.benefit2.title: Sound
headphones.benefit3.title: Comfort
headphones.benefit4.title: Battery
headphones.pros1: Best noise cancellation on the market — total isolation
headphones.pros2: Neural network voice processor for perfect calls
headphones.pros3: 30 hours of battery — enough for a work week
headphones.pros4: Light and comfortable for all-day wear
headphones.pros5: Fast charging: 3 minutes = 3 hours of use
headphones.cons2: Case takes up a lot of space in a bag
headphones.cons4: No aptX support (only LDAC for Android)
monitor.setup_advice_title: How to Set Up Your Monitor for Work
